perturbation.original_text: The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Definitive Agreement"), such information will be kept confidential, except that each party may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its directors, officers, employees, consultants, advisors and professional representatives (the persons to whom such disclosure is permissible being collectively called "Representatives") in each case who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies; provided , however , that prior to disclosing the Evaluation Material or any portion thereof to any of such Representatives, the disclosing party will secure the undertaking of its Representative to be bound by the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement to the same extent that the disclosing party is bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Final Accord"), such information will be kept confidential, except that each party may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its directors, officers, employees, consultants, advisors and professional representatives (the persons to whom such disclosure is permissible being collectively called "Representatives") in each case who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies; provided , however , that prior to disclosing the Evaluation Material or any portion thereof to any of such Representatives, the disclosing party will secure the undertaking of its Representative to be bound by the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement to the same extent that the disclosing party is bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Definitive Agreement' to 'Final Accord' creates confusion. The term Definitive agreement is being used again later in the paragraph and also on other sections. This undermines the legal precision required, leaving room to disputes over the stage at which parties are legally bound, leading to a contradiction within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies and not in any way directly or indirectly detrimental to the Companies (competitively or otherwise), and unless and until the parties have completed a transaction pursuant to a formal contractually binding agreement ("Final Accord"), such information will be kept confidential, except that each party may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its directors, officers, employees, consultants, advisors and professional representatives (the persons to whom such disclosure is permissible being collectively called "Representatives") in each case who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the Companies; provided , however , that prior to disclosing the Evaluation Material or any portion thereof to any of such Representatives, the disclosing party will secure the undertaking of its Representative to be bound by the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement to the same extent that the disclosing party is bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Definitive Agreement' to 'Final Accord' introduces misaligned terminology. While the altered sentence is repeated in the contradicted text, the core issue is that the contract now uses two different terms ('Definitive Agreement' elsewhere, as the explanation indicates, and 'Final Accord' here) to refer to what appears to be the same concept (a formal, contractually binding agreement). This creates ambiguity and potential for disputes regarding when the parties are legally bound. This directly aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definition, leading to conflicting interpretations and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to and known by the public (other than in the case of Evaluation Material for Scientific Games as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by MDI or its Representatives and in the case of Evaluation Material for MDI as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by Scientific Games or its Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to Scientific Games or MDI on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the other party or its advisors, provided that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with or for the benefit of the party with respect to whom the information pertains, or (iii) was in the possession of the disclosing party prior to the date of this Agreement and was obtained by such party without the breach, directly or indirectly, by such party or any other person of any obligation or duty owed to the party with respect to whom the information pertains.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to and known by the public (other than in the case of Evaluation Material for Scientific Games as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by MDI or its Representatives and in the case of Evaluation Material for MDI as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by Scientific Games or its Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to Scientific Games or MDI on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the other party or its advisors, provided that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality undertaking with or for the benefit of the party with respect to whom the information pertains, or (iii) was in the possession of the disclosing party prior to the date of this Agreement and was obtained by such party without the breach, directly or indirectly, by such party or any other person of any obligation or duty owed to the party with respect to whom the information pertains.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidentiality agreement' to 'confidentiality undertaking' affects the scope of excluded information. While the final part still mentions 'breach', it is not clear whether a simple undertaking has the same legal standing as a 'agreement', that leads to a confusion. The second is a legally binding contract, while an undertaking is more of a promise without clearly defined repercussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include any information which (i) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to and known by the public (other than in the case of Evaluation Material for Scientific Games as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by MDI or its Representatives and in the case of Evaluation Material for MDI as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by Scientific Games or its Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to Scientific Games or MDI on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the other party or its advisors, provided that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality undertaking with or for the benefit of the party with respect to whom the information pertains, or (iii) was in the possession of the disclosing party prior to the date of this Agreement and was obtained by such party without the breach, directly or indirectly, by such party or any other person of any obligation or duty owed to the party with respect to whom the information pertains.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidentiality agreement' to 'confidentiality undertaking' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material' creates a contradiction. While both the changed text and the contradicted text contain this altered definition, the change itself introduces uncertainty. An 'agreement' has a stronger legal standing than an 'undertaking.' This difference in legal enforceability directly impacts whether a 'breach' (mentioned in clause iii) has occurred. The original definition assumed an agreement where breach has clear legal implications, but an undertaking does not. This creates a conflict regarding the scope of protected information. The original text relied on the breached of agreement, whereas the change text is unclear as to whether an undertaking even constitutes an agreement that is breached.
